title: Space Shooter
description: A 2D top-down space shooter arcade game with multi-input player control, laser projectile pooling, AABB collision detection, wave-based difficulty scaling, particle explosion FX, and localStorage high-score persistence.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Multi-input starship control: mousemove/touchmove with coordinate remap for absolute positioning, arrow/WASD key-state map for incremental steering, all clamped to [0, W-w] × [0, H-h]
Laser projectile pool: shootLaser() creates 4×14 rects at player nose with speed=7, cooldown=8 frames, culled at y+h < 0 via splice
Enemy spawn queue: diamond-shaped enemies with randomized size (22-34×20-30), speed = 0.8 + wave×0.15, spawn rate = max(18, 50 - wave×2), culled at y > H+20
Dual AABB collision passes: laser-vs-enemy (destroy both + particle burst + score), enemy-vs-player (decrement hull + screen shake + explosion)
Wave system: wave = floor(score/200) + 1, drives enemy speed and spawn rate, displayed in telemetry
Particle engine: spawnExplosion() creates 10-14 particles with random angular velocity, gravity vy+=0.04, alpha decay life-=decay per frame
Hull integrity: MAX_HULL=5, unicode block character display █ per point, shake animation on hit, <=0 triggers gameOver()
Scrolling starfield: 80 stars with randomized size/speed/alpha, y+=speed per frame, recycled to top when exceeding H
localStorage ssBest key: written on destruction if score exceeds stored record, displayed on boot
Canvas resize handler: re-maps buffer to CSS bounding rect dimensions for collision accuracy across viewports
